title: Public Transport Operators
description: Belgium's four regional public transport operators.

tips: Book via the SNCB app or belgiantrain.be for live platform info.
Trains connect all major cities; Brussels has three main stations: Midi/Zuid, Central, and Nord/Noord.
Buy tickets before boarding where possible — on-board purchase carries a surcharge.
Tap in with a MOBIB card or contactless bank card on buses and trams; validate paper tickets at metro gates.
The Brupass and Brupass XL allow combined travel with De Lijn, TEC and SNCB within Brussels.
Metro runs roughly 06:00-00:00, with night buses (Noctis) on weekends.
The De Lijn app is the cheapest way to buy tickets — in-vehicle purchase costs more.
Antwerp and Ghent have extensive tram networks operated by De Lijn.
Buy tickets via the TEC app before boarding for the best price.
Coverage is bus-focused outside Charleroi's light rail network.
